basic_details.job_title: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 82 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) has released the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 notification (Advertisement No. 433/2026-27) for 82 X-Ray Assistant, Class-3 vacancies under the Health and Family Welfare Department. Candidates aged 18 to 33 years with HSC (Physics, Chemistry, English) qualification can apply online at ojas.gujarat.gov.in from 20 July 2026 (2:00 PM) to 3 August 2026 (11:59 PM). Selected candidates will draw a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 for the first five years.

application_fee.details: The application fee for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 differs for unreserved and reserved category candidates and must be paid online only. | The fee must be paid online through Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallet after the application is confirmed on OJAS, and the last date to pay the exam fee is 6 August 2026 (11:59 PM). Candidates who appear for the examination will get the fee refunded through the same payment mode used, but no refund is given to candidates who do not appear for the exam. Applications for which the fee is not paid are automatically rejected without prior notice, and the fee is non-refundable even if the application itself is later cancelled by the board.

age_limit.details: The age of the candidate must be not less than 18 years and not more than 33 years as on 3 August 2026 . Reserved category candidates, women, ex-servicemen, and PwD candidates are eligible for age relaxation as per the table below. | Category Relaxation Maximum Age Limit General category women 5 years - Reserved category men 5 years - Reserved category women 10 years (5+5) 45 years General category PwD men 10 years 45 years General category PwD women 15 years (10+5) 45 years Reserved category PwD men 15 years (5+10) 45 years Reserved category PwD women 20 years (5+10+5) 45 years Ex-servicemen Service period + 3 years - | Candidates already in Gujarat Government service may also get relaxation in the upper age limit as per the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Reservation benefits and age relaxation for SC, ST, SEBC, and EWS candidates apply only to candidates who are originally from Gujarat.

qualification.details: Applicants often ask what qualifications are needed for the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant post - the answer covers both educational qualification and age limit, detailed below. | Candidates applying for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 must have passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate (HSC/12th) examination with Physics, Chemistry, and English as subjects, conducted by the Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board. This qualification standard has been fixed as per the Health and Family Welfare Department's resolution dated 18 November 2024. In addition, candidates must possess basic computer knowledge as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification (General) Rules, 1967, and adequate knowledge of Gujarati and/or Hindi. | Candidates who do not currently hold a computer knowledge certificate can still apply, but they must submit proof of having passed a basic computer knowledge test before the appointing authority prior to appointment - failure to do so will make the candidate ineligible for appointment, and the selection can be cancelled by the appointing authority. | The age of the candidate must be not less than 18 years and not more than 33 years as on 3 August 2026. Reserved category candidates, women, ex-servicemen, and PwD candidates are eligible for age relaxation as per the table below. | Candidates already in Gujarat Government service may also get relaxation in the upper age limit as per the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Reservation benefits and age relaxation for SC, ST, SEBC, and EWS candidates apply only to candidates who are originally from Gujarat.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 will initially be appointed on a fixed pay before moving to the regular pay matrix. During the first five years, appointees will receive a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 as per the Finance Department's resolutions. After five years, subject to satisfactory service, candidates will be placed in the regular Pay Level-2 of the 7th Pay Commission, ranging from ₹19,900 to ₹63,200 per month, along with other applicable benefits under the relevant government resolutions. This regularization is subject to the outcome of pending SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012 before the Supreme Court.

selection_process: Part-A Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension 30
Selection for the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant post is based on a single-stage computer-based/OMR written examination followed by document verification
so candidates do not need to appear for multiple test stages. The exam consists of objective-type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) split into Part-A and Part-B.
The combined duration for Part-A and Part-B is 3 hours (180 minutes)
and the exam follows negative marking - 1/4th mark is deducted for every wrong answer. A minimum qualifying standard of 40% applies independently to both Part-A and Part-B for all categories
meaning candidates must clear both sections separately to remain in contention.
GSSSB will release a provisional answer key
and then publish the final answer key followed by the provisional result. Roughly twice the number of vacancies per category will be called for document verification based on merit
after which the final selection list and waiting list will be published. While exact dates for the exam and result are not yet announced
candidates can expect this stage to follow a few months after the application window closes
based on GSSSB's usual recruitment timelines.
how_to_apply: Candidates can complete the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Apply Online 2026 process by following the steps below on the OJAS portal.
Click on "Online Application," select "Apply," and choose GSSSB.
Click "Apply Now," then "Skip" to open the application format, and fill in Personal Details first.
Accept the terms under "Assurance" by selecting "Yes" and click "Save" to complete the application.
Note down the Application Number generated after saving.
Upload your photograph and signature - photo size should be 5 cm height x 3.6 cm width, and signature should be 2.5 cm height x 7.5 cm width, both in JPG format not exceeding 15 KB each.

Go to "Confirm Application" under the Online Application tab, enter your Application Number and Date of Birth, review all details carefully, and click "Confirm Application." No changes are allowed after confirmation.
Note the Confirmation Number generated - this is required for fee payment and printing the form.
Pay the application fee online, then download and print the confirmation receipt and the application form for future reference.

Candidates should apply only once; if multiple applications are submitted, only the last confirmed application with the correct fee paid will be considered valid, and fees paid against earlier applications will not be refunded or adjusted.

content_sections.heading: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Vacancy Details 2026 (82 Posts)
content_sections.text: The GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 covers a total of 82 vacancies , split across two recruiting offices - the Office of the Additional Director, Medical Services, Gandhinagar ( 76 posts ) and the Employees' State Insurance (ESI) Scheme Office, Ahmedabad ( 6 posts ). The table below shows the category-wise breakup for both offices. Office of the Additional Director, Medical Services, Gandhinagar - 76 Posts ESI Scheme Office, Ahmedabad - 6 Posts Combined Total (Both Offices)

tables.caption: Office of the Additional Director, Medical Services, Gandhinagar - 76 Posts

content_sections.heading: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Applicants often ask what qualifications are needed for the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant post - the answer covers both educational qualification and age limit, detailed below. Candidates applying for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 must have passed the Higher Secondary School Certificate (HSC/12th) examination with Physics, Chemistry, and English as subjects, conducted by the Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board. This qualification standard has been fixed as per the Health and Family Welfare Department's resolution dated 18 November 2024. In addition, candidates must possess basic computer knowledge as prescribed under the Gujarat Civil Services Classification (General) Rules, 1967, and adequate knowledge of Gujarati and/or Hindi . Candidates who do not currently hold a computer knowledge certificate can still apply, but they must submit proof of having passed a basic computer knowledge test before the appointing authority prior to appointment - failure to do so will make the candidate ineligible for appointment, and the selection can be cancelled by the appointing authority. The age of the candidate must be not less than 18 years and not more than 33 years as on 3 August 2026 . Reserved category candidates, women, ex-servicemen, and PwD candidates are eligible for age relaxation as per the table below. Candidates already in Gujarat Government service may also get relaxation in the upper age limit as per the Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. Reservation benefits and age relaxation for SC, ST, SEBC, and EWS candidates apply only to candidates who are originally from Gujarat.

content_sections.heading: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 differs for unreserved and reserved category candidates and must be paid online only. The fee must be paid online through Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallet after the application is confirmed on OJAS, and the last date to pay the exam fee is 6 August 2026 (11:59 PM) . Candidates who appear for the examination will get the fee refunded through the same payment mode used, but no refund is given to candidates who do not appear for the exam. Applications for which the fee is not paid are automatically rejected without prior notice, and the fee is non-refundable even if the application itself is later cancelled by the board.

rows.category: Reserved (all categories of women, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, Ex-servicemen)

content_sections.heading: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection for the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant post is based on a single-stage computer-based/OMR written examination followed by document verification, so candidates do not need to appear for multiple test stages. The exam consists of objective-type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) split into Part-A and Part-B. The combined duration for Part-A and Part-B is 3 hours (180 minutes) , and the exam follows negative marking - 1/4th mark is deducted for every wrong answer. A minimum qualifying standard of 40% applies independently to both Part-A and Part-B for all categories, meaning candidates must clear both sections separately to remain in contention. After the exam, GSSSB will release a provisional answer key, invite objections, and then publish the final answer key followed by the provisional result. Roughly twice the number of vacancies per category will be called for document verification based on merit, after which the final selection list and waiting list will be published. While exact dates for the exam and result are not yet announced, candidates can expect this stage to follow a few months after the application window closes, based on GSSSB's usual recruitment timelines.

rows.subject: Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates can complete the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Apply Online 2026 process by following the steps below on the OJAS portal. Visit the official website ojas.gujarat.gov.in . Click on "Online Application," select "Apply," and choose GSSSB . Select and click Apply. Click "Apply Now," then "Skip" to open the application format, and fill in Personal Details first. Fill in Educational Details next. Accept the terms under "Assurance" by selecting "Yes" and click "Save" to complete the application. Note down the Application Number generated after saving. Upload your photograph and signature - photo size should be 5 cm height x 3.6 cm width , and signature should be 2.5 cm height x 7.5 cm width , both in JPG format not exceeding 15 KB each. Go to "Confirm Application" under the Online Application tab, enter your Application Number and Date of Birth, review all details carefully, and click "Confirm Application." No changes are allowed after confirmation. Note the Confirmation Number generated - this is required for fee payment and printing the form. Pay the application fee online, then download and print the confirmation receipt and the application form for future reference. Candidates should apply only once; if multiple applications are submitted, only the last confirmed application with the correct fee paid will be considered valid, and fees paid against earlier applications will not be refunded or adjusted.

content_sections.heading: GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026 will initially be appointed on a fixed pay before moving to the regular pay matrix. During the first five years , appointees will receive a fixed monthly pay of ₹26,000 as per the Finance Department's resolutions. After five years, subject to satisfactory service, candidates will be placed in the regular Pay Level-2 of the 7th Pay Commission, ranging from ₹19,900 to ₹63,200 per month , along with other applicable benefits under the relevant government resolutions. This regularization is subject to the outcome of pending SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012 before the Supreme Court.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must keep all original certificates ready before applying and during the later document verification stage. Required documents include the HSC/12th mark sheet showing Physics, Chemistry, and English, the school leaving certificate for age proof, category certificate (SC/ST/SEBC/EWS, where applicable, obtained within the validity period specified in the notification), computer knowledge certificate (if available), disability certificate (for PwD candidates, in the format prescribed by the Health and Family Welfare Department circular dated 15 December 2025), ex-servicemen identity card and discharge book (if applicable), and a recent passport-size photograph with white background, taken within one year of the last date of application. Widow candidates claiming the 5% merit weightage must also submit proof of not having remarried at the time of appointment, whenever asked by the board.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates lose out on valid applications due to avoidable errors rather than ineligibility, so it helps to double-check the form carefully before confirming it. First, always verify your category (caste/SEBC/EWS) selection carefully - GSSSB does not entertain any request to change the category after the application is confirmed. Second, upload your photo and signature exactly as per the specified size and file format (JPG, under 15 KB), since a mismatched photo across stages can cause identification problems later. Third, do not confirm the application until every field has been cross-checked against your original certificates, because no correction is possible once the application is confirmed - you would need to submit a fresh application with a new confirmation number and pay the fee again before the last date. Fourth, remember that paying the fee is a separate, mandatory step after confirming the application; an unpaid application is treated as invalid and rejected without notice. Finally, keep your registered mobile number and email ID active throughout the recruitment process, as GSSSB sends all exam-related updates via SMS and email to the details submitted in the form.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window is open from 20 July 2026 (2:00 PM) to 3 August 2026 (11:59 PM). The exam fee must be paid by 6 August 2026 (11:59 PM).

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 82 vacancies - 76 under the Additional Director, Medical Services, Gandhinagar, and 6 under the ESI Scheme Office, Ahmedabad.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed HSC (12th) with Physics, Chemistry, and English, along with basic computer knowledge and knowledge of Gujarati and/or Hindi.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 18 and 33 years of age as on 3 August 2026, with age relaxation available for reserved categories, women, ex-servicemen, and PwD candidates as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹500 for unreserved category candidates and ₹400 for reserved category candidates (including all women, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, PwD, and ex-servicemen candidates).

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant?
faq.answer: Selection is based on a single computer-based/OMR written exam of 210 marks (Part-A: 90 marks, Part-B: 120 marks) with negative marking, followed by document verification and a final merit list.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant post?
faq.answer: Selected candidates get a fixed pay of ₹26,000 per month for the first five years, after which they move to Pay Level-2 (₹19,900–₹63,200) of the 7th Pay Commission.

faq.question: Q8. How can I apply online for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply through ojas.gujarat.gov.in by selecting GSSSB, choosing Advertisement No. 433/2026-27, and completing the Personal Details, Educational Details, photo/signature upload, and application confirmation steps.

faq.question: Q9. s there negative marking in the GSSSB X-Ray Assistant exam?
faq.answer: Yes, 1/4th mark is deducted for every wrong answer in the MCQ-based written examination.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official websites are ojas.gujarat.gov.in (for applying) and gsssb.gujarat.gov.in (for the notification and updates).

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for GSSSB X-Ray Assistant Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window is open from 20 July 2026 (2:00 PM) to 3 August 2026 (11:59 PM). The exam fee must be paid by 6 August 2026 (11:59 PM).
